hook: I lost 4 hours last week looking for a note I took on my phone
texts: I took a note during a meeting. A good one. Gone.
Searched everywhere — notes app, photos, voice memos, browser history. Nothing.
Turns out I'd left it in a completely different app I forgot I'd used that day.
That was the moment I decided to fix this once and for all.
caption: If you've ever lost a note you actually needed 😭 #IndieApp #Productivity #AppDev

hook: I checked my phone 94 times yesterday and got almost nothing done
texts: I turned on screen time tracking as a joke.
94 unlocks. 4.5 hours of screen time. And my to-do list barely moved.
Most of those unlocks were to check something I'd already seen.
The problem isn't willpower — it's that there's no system.
caption: Screen time numbers don't lie 😬 #Productivity #DigitalWellness #IndieApp

hook: My best ideas always come during my commute and disappear before I get home
texts: On the train. Great idea. I'll remember it. I will definitely remember it.
I did not remember it.
I've started treating every commute like a creative session — capture everything, review later.
The ideas didn't get better. I just stopped losing them.
caption: The commute-to-desk idea graveyard is real 🪦 #IndieApp #AppDev #ProductivityHack

hook: I published my first app and got 3 downloads in the first week — one was my mom
texts: Year one of indie dev is a masterclass in humility.
3 downloads. 3. And I'd spent 6 months building the thing.
What I didn't know then: the app was fine, the marketing was invisible.
The product is only half the job.
caption: Indie dev origin story 😅 #IndieAppDev #AppStore #BuildInPublic #iOSDev

hook: I used to dread Mondays until I figured out the 20-minute Sunday reset
texts: Sunday evening. Open the app. 20 minutes.
Review what happened last week. Note what carried over. Set 3 priorities for Monday.
Not a full review, not a life audit — just enough to start Monday with direction.
That's it. 20 minutes and Mondays feel completely different.
caption: 20-minute Sunday reset changed my whole week 🔄 #Productivity #SundayReset #IndieApp

hook: My meeting notes are a crime scene — scattered across 4 apps and 3 notebooks
texts: Google Docs for the important ones. Apple Notes for the quick ones.
Voice memo for when I couldn't type fast enough. Slack DM to myself when I was lazy.
Action items? Somewhere in there. Good luck finding them.
The system that works is the one you'll actually use consistently.
caption: Meeting notes archaeology 🕵️ #ProductivityHack #MeetingNotes #AppDev

hook: I spent $200 on ads and got 2 downloads — here's what I learned
texts: Ran a campaign. Set it up carefully. Watched the dashboard.
$200 later: 2 downloads, 0 reviews, and a lot of lessons.
The problem wasn't the ad. It was that I had no idea who I was talking to.
Paid traffic amplifies what's already there — organic first.
caption: Expensive lesson on day 60 of indie dev 💸 #IndieAppDev #AppMarketing #BuildInPublic

hook: The most stressful part of shipping an app isn't the code — it's the waiting
texts: App submitted. Review pending. Nothing to do but wait.
You find 3 bugs you want to fix. You can't. It's out of your hands.
Apple approves it. Users download it. And then you hold your breath again.
Shipping never gets less stressful. You just get better at sitting with it.
caption: The post-submit waiting game 😰 #iOSDev #AppStore #IndieAppDev #SwiftUI

hook: The people I know who are the most productive don't have better tools — they have smaller goals
texts: I used to set massive weekly goals. I hit maybe half.
I switched to daily goals of 3 things. I hit most of them.
Small wins are underrated. They compound. They build confidence.
Ambition is good. But it works better in small doses, every day.
caption: Small wins > big goals 🎯 #Productivity #SmallWins #IndieApp #AppDev

hook: People ask why I do indie dev when it would be easier to just work at a company
texts: Honest answer: it would be easier. The pay, the stability, the perks.
But I'd be building someone else's thing, for someone else's users, toward someone else's goals.
Indie dev is harder. It's also mine.
Every user who finds the app useful — that's a direct line from me to them. No middleman.
caption: Why indie dev despite everything 🛠️ #IndieAppDev #BuildInPublic #iOSDev #SoloFounder
